case.title: Jack the Ripper
case.mystery: a killer vanished behind letters, fog, and split police jurisdictions
case.clue: the infamous letters were likely hoaxes that distracted police and press
case.twist: the most famous suspect lists may rest on myths and media hype
case.caution: Avoid naming one person as the killer; the case is unsolved.

segments.label: Hook
segments.title: Fog and fear
segments.voiceover: Whitechapel, 1888. Five women murdered. The killer? Never found.
segments.visual: Foggy alley, map pin on Whitechapel; sepia streetlamps flicker.
segments.caption: Fog. Fear.

factCheck.note: Murders occurred in Whitechapel, 1888; case remains unsolved. (Britannica; National Archives)

segments.label: Timeline
segments.title: Autumn of terror
segments.voiceover: From August to November, fear gripped London's East End as attacks escalated.
segments.visual: Calendar pages flip: Aug-Nov 1888; East End streets dissolve behind.
segments.caption: East End panic

factCheck.note: Key murders span Aug-Nov 1888. (Whitechapel murders timeline)

segments.label: Jurisdictions
segments.title: A split hunt
segments.voiceover: Two forces-Met and City police-split jurisdiction, complicating the hunt.
segments.visual: Animated boundary line; MET vs CITY badges on either side.
segments.caption: Split chase

factCheck.note: Crimes fell under both Metropolitan Police and City of London Police. (National Archives; Wikipedia)

segments.label: Letters
segments.title: Ink and misdirection
segments.voiceover: Newspapers splashed 'Dear Boss' and 'From Hell.' Most letters were likely hoaxes.
segments.visual: Close-ups of facsimile headlines; red wax seal; letters blur out.
segments.caption: Hoax letters?

factCheck.note: Historians largely agree most Ripper letters were hoaxes. (National Archives; Casebook)

segments.label: Graffito
segments.title: Clue erased
segments.voiceover: A chalk graffito near a torn apron piece was erased by Commissioner Warren.
segments.visual: Brick wall text fades as a hand wipes chalk; alley sign: Goulston Street.
segments.caption: Erased clue

factCheck.note: Goulston Street graffito erased on Warren's order after apron piece found nearby. (Goulston Street graffito page; archives)

segments.label: Victims list
segments.title: 'Canonical five'
segments.voiceover: The 'canonical five' label came later; which murders belong remains debated.
segments.visual: Five silhouettes; subtle text: Nichols, Chapman, Stride, Eddowes, Kelly.
segments.caption: Debated list

factCheck.note: 'Canonical five' is a retrospective construct; inclusion is debated. (National Archives; Britannica)

segments.label: Forensics
segments.title: Tools of the time
segments.voiceover: No fingerprints or DNA; crowds often disturbed scenes before proper cordons.
segments.visual: Victorian crowd illustration overlay; no modern tape, officers hold back onlookers.
segments.caption: No forensics

factCheck.note: Forensics then lacked fingerprints/DNA; scene control was limited. (Britannica; policing histories)

segments.label: Media
segments.title: Panic sells
segments.voiceover: Sensational papers fueled panic; some argue coverage helped the killer disappear.
segments.visual: Rotating broadsheets; headlines enlarge, then fade to black.
segments.caption: Media frenzy

factCheck.note: Press sensationalism is well documented; effect on escape is speculative. (National Archives; secondary analyses)

segments.label: Suspects
segments.title: Myths multiply
segments.voiceover: Famous suspect lists lean on myths; many 'confession' letters were fakes.
segments.visual: Suspect board dissolves; crossed-out letters fall away.
segments.caption: Suspects? Myths.

factCheck.note: Fake letters are well attested; quality of suspect lists varies widely. (Casebook; National Archives)

segments.label: Close
segments.title: Enduring riddle
segments.voiceover: Unsolved to this day. Which clue misled us-letters, graffito, or chaos?
segments.visual: Night alley fades; question cards: Letters? Graffito? Chaos?
segments.caption: Still unsolved

factCheck.note: Case remains officially unsolved. (Britannica; National Archives)

sources.title: The National Archives (UK) - Jack the Ripper
sources.note: Official records overview; letters generally considered hoaxes; policing context and timeline.

sources.title: Encyclopaedia Britannica - Jack the Ripper
sources.note: Summary of the murders, victims, and the case remaining unsolved.

sources.title: Casebook: Jack the Ripper - The Letters
sources.note: Documents the Ripper correspondence; scholarly consensus that most letters were hoaxes.

sources.title: Wikipedia - Whitechapel murders
sources.note: Useful consolidated timeline and noting Met vs City police jurisdictions; cross-check with other sources.

sources.title: Wikipedia - Goulston Street graffito
sources.note: Details on the graffito and Commissioner Warren's erasure; corroborated by archival sources.
